title: Design System: The Legends of Essolis

display.purpose: Page hero titles — used in .page-hero__title. IM Fell English. Amber Wax Seal color.

headline.purpose: Major screen headings like the login title. IM Fell English.

title.purpose: Modal headers, item names in expanded view, character names. IM Fell English. Amber or Old Ivory.

body.purpose: Card descriptions, lore entries, running prose. Garamond. Cap at 65ch in prose contexts.

label.purpose: Metadata only: badges, form field headers, rarity chips. Sans-serif, ALL CAPS, 10-11px. Never IM Fell English in all-caps.

shadows.name: panel-ambient
shadows.purpose: Cards and panel containers at rest.

shadows.name: hero-diffuse
shadows.purpose: Hero containers and login card — always present.

shadows.name: nav-bleed
shadows.purpose: Sticky nav bar. Anchors to the top of the viewport.

shadows.name: amber-glow-low
shadows.purpose: Subtle amber glow on primary button hover and selected items.

shadows.name: amber-glow-high
shadows.purpose: Attuned item card border glow, loading state pulse.

shadows.name: attune-ring
shadows.purpose: Full attuned item card shadow system.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions: nav underlines, button hover, color changes.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Entrance animations: login card slide-in, modal open, card entrance.

motion.name: login-card-in
motion.purpose: Login card entrance animation on page load.

motion.name: login-btn-glow
motion.purpose: Loading state on the login button.

motion.name: card-in
motion.value: opacity 0 -> 1, translateY(8px) scale(0.97) -> translateY(0) scale(1), 0.22s ease-out
motion.purpose: Character card entrance when grid renders.

motion.name: modal-in
motion.value: opacity 0 -> 1, scale(0.96) translateY(12px) -> scale(1) translateY(0), 0.2s ease-out

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Nav collapses to hamburger drawer. Section padding reduces. Touch target minimum 44px enforced.

breakpoints.name: mobile-sm
breakpoints.purpose: Character grid collapses to 2 columns. Inventory modal slides up from bottom.

breakpoints.name: mobile-xs
breakpoints.purpose: Character grid collapses to single column.

breakpoints.name: inventory-mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Inventory-specific: grid goes single column, compact 3-column view toggle appears.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main action button. Dark brown gradient with amber border. Used in modals and forms.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #3a2008 0%, #5a3410 100%); border: 1px solid #c8a45c; border-radius: 7px; color: #ffcc66; font-family: 'Garamond', serif; font-size: 14px; padding: 9px 22px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #4a2c0e 0%, #6a4018 100%); box-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(200,164,92,0.2); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.96); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: default; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffcc66; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: 7px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary/cancel button. Transparent with leather border. Recedes until needed.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: none; border: 1px solid #3a2510; border-radius: 7px; color: #888; font-family: 'Garamond', serif; font-size: 14px; padding: 9px 22px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: #6a4a2a; color: #f4e6d7; } .ds-btn-ghost:active { transform: scale(0.96); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffcc66; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: DM Action Button
components.description: Admin/DM-only action. Amber ghost style — visible but not intrusive for player-facing views.
components.css: .ds-btn-dm { font-family: 'Garamond', serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: bold; letter-spacing: 0.03em; padding: 8px 18px; background: rgba(255,204,102,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(255,204,102,0.4); color: #ffcc66; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, border-color 0.15s, box-shadow 0.18s, transform 0.1s; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-btn-dm:hover { background: rgba(255,204,102,0.22); border-color: #ffcc66; box-shadow: 0 0 14px rgba(255,204,102,0.22); } .ds-btn-dm:active { transform: scale(0.96); } .ds-btn-dm:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffcc66; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Filter Tab
components.description: Inventory and lore filter tabs. Pill shape; amber on active. Default and active shown.
components.css: .ds-filter-tabs { display: flex; gap: 6px; flex-wrap: wrap; } .ds-filter-tab { background: transparent; border: 1px solid #3a2510; border-radius: 20px; color: #888; font-family: 'Garamond', serif; font-size: 13px; padding: 5px 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.2s, color 0.2s, background 0.2s; } .ds-filter-tab:hover { border-color: #ffcc66; color: #ffcc66; } .ds-filter-tab--active { background: rgba(255,204,102,0.1); border-color: #ffcc66; color: #ffcc66; } .ds-filter-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffcc66; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Form Input
components.description: Standard form input. Near-invisible background, leather border, amber focus. 16px font required.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 5px; } .ds-label { font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #888; font-family: sans-serif; } .ds-input { width: 100%; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.04); border: 1px solid #3a2510; border-radius: 8px; padding: 9px 12px; color: #f4e6d7; font-size: 16px; font-family: 'Garamond', serif; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #ffcc66; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #555; }

components.name: Item Card
components.description: Inventory item card. Rarity-tinted border and background via --rc. Ambient shadow at rest, hover deepens.
components.html:
⚔️

Longsword +1

WeaponUncommon

A finely crafted blade with a keen edge, slightly enchanted.
components.css: .ds-item-card { --rc: #3a2510; background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--rc) 22%, #1a1008); border: 1px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--rc) 65%, #1a1a1a); border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; padding: 14px 16px 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; font-family: 'Garamond', serif; box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); } .ds-item-card:hover { border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--rc) 70%, #1a1a1a); box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); } .ds-item-card__top { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-item-card__icon { font-size: 22px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-item-card__name { font-family: 'IM Fell English', 'Garamond', serif; font-size: 16px; color: #f4e6d7; margin: 0 0 4px; } .ds-item-card__badges { display: flex; gap: 5px; flex-wrap: wrap; } .ds-badge { font-size: 10px; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.07em; padding: 2px 7px; border-radius: 10px; font-family: sans-serif; } .ds-badge--weapon { background: rgba(224,80,80,0.12); border: 1px solid #e05050; color: #e05050; } .ds-badge--uncommon { background: rgba(76,175,80,0.12); border: 1px solid #4caf50; color: #4caf50; } .ds-item-card__desc { font-size: 13px; color: #999; line-height: 1.45; margin: 0; }

components.name: Rarity Chip Strip
components.description: The five rarity chips rendered inline. Used on item cards, filter controls, and add-item modals.
components.html: CommonUncommonRareVery RareLegendary
components.css: .ds-rarity-chips { display: flex; gap: 6px; flex-wrap: wrap; } .ds-rarity-chip { font-size: 11px; border-radius: 10px; padding: 1px 8px; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.04em; } .ds-rarity-chip--common { background: rgba(158,158,158,0.12); border: 1px solid #9e9e9e; color: #9e9e9e; } .ds-rarity-chip--uncommon { background: rgba(76,175,80,0.12); border: 1px solid #4caf50; color: #4caf50; } .ds-rarity-chip--rare { background: rgba(33,150,243,0.12); border: 1px solid #2196f3; color: #2196f3; } .ds-rarity-chip--very-rare { background: rgba(156,39,176,0.12); border: 1px solid #9c27b0; color: #9c27b0; } .ds-rarity-chip--legendary { background: rgba(255,152,0,0.12); border: 1px solid #ff9800; color: #ff9800; }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Navigation link with animated underline. Animated scaleX on hover/active using ease-out-quart.
components.html: InventoryMissionsLore
components.css: .ds-nav-demo { display: flex; gap: 4px; background: linear-gradient(180deg, #2a1505 0%, #1c0e03 100%); padding: 0 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid #3a2510; } .ds-nav-link { text-decoration: none; font-size: 15px; font-family: 'Garamond', serif; letter-spacing: 0.06em; color: #c9a87a; padding: 14px 18px; display: block; position: relative; transition: color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-nav-link::after { content: ''; position: absolute; bottom: 0; left: 18px; right: 18px; height: 2px; background: #ffcc66; border-radius: 2px 2px 0 0; transform: scaleX(0); transition: transform 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #ffcc66; } .ds-nav-link:hover::after { transform: scaleX(1); } .ds-nav-link--active { color: #ffcc66; } .ds-nav-link--active::after { transform: scaleX(1); } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffcc66; outline-offset: -4px; }

narrative.overview:
This is a D&D campaign companion used during live sessions at the table. Everything on the surface has a reason to be there. The palette is deep brown and amber because those are the colors of leather bindings, ink on vellum, and a single candle over old paper — not as a genre gesture, but as the literal physical context of use. The fonts are serious without being theatrical. IM Fell English carries the weight of a world with 400 years of accumulated history; Garamond moves text without calling attention to itself. The system doesn't gesture at a fantasy world — it exists inside one.

The UI recedes where it should and surfaces what matters. Spacing and typographic contrast do the structural work; color accents signal action and status, never structure. A crowded surface is not more atmospheric — it is harder to read at 11pm when someone asks 'wait, do I have that scroll?'

This system explicitly rejects: generic SaaS dashboards (white card grids, blue primary actions, Inter/system fonts), children's fantasy aesthetics (bright primary colors, rounded bubble fonts, playful cartoonish ornaments), and anything that reads like a spreadsheet wearing a costume.
keyCharacteristics: Single amber accent (Amber Wax Seal) against a cascade of brown-black neutrals from vault to near-void
Serif display type (IM Fell English) for headings; Garamond for body; sans-serif strictly for metadata labels and badges
Ambient shadow present at rest on cards; elevated shadow on hover; heavy structural shadow for modals
Rarity as a semantic color system (common through legendary) — five distinct hues, never repurposed
Touch-first on mobile: 44px minimum targets, 16px minimum font sizes on all inputs

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Amber Wax Seal is used on 10% or less of any screen at rest. Reach for Old Ivory or Muted Smoke first; amber marks action and current state, nothing else.

rules.name: The Rarity Covenant
rules.body: The five rarity colors exist solely for item and spell rarity. Never borrow a rarity color for another status, category, or decorative role.

rules.name: The Serif Hierarchy Rule
rules.body: IM Fell English marks importance (headings, titles, named things). Garamond carries content (body, buttons, nav links). Sans-serif signals metadata (badges, caps labels, stat rows). Mix them only within these assignments.

rules.name: The Label Case Rule
rules.body: Uppercase + letter-spacing is reserved for metadata chips, form field headers, and structural labels. Running prose is never uppercased.

rules.name: The Glow Restriction
rules.body: The amber glow shadow is reserved for semantically active surfaces: attuned items, loading animations, confirmed selections. It is not a hover affordance for general cards or buttons.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule for Inputs
rules.body: Form inputs carry no shadow at rest. Focus is communicated by the border shifting to Amber Wax Seal alone — the border change is the signal.

dos: Do use Amber Wax Seal (#ffcc66) exclusively for interactive signals: active states, focus borders, action CTAs. Let Old Ivory (#f4e6d7) carry text.
Do keep the dark-to-darker gradient pattern for hero sections, nav, and modal headers (from ~#2a1505 toward #1c0e03 or #181008).
Do use IM Fell English for headings and Garamond for body text. Reserve sans-serif for metadata labels, badges, and stat chips at 10-11px.
Do enforce min-height: 44px on every interactive element rendered on mobile.
Do enforce font-size: 16px minimum on all input, select, and textarea elements to prevent iOS auto-zoom.
Do use the rarity color system (common through legendary) for item and spell rarity only.
Do use var(--ease-out-quart) or var(--ease-out-expo) for all UI transitions. No bounce, no elastic, no linear.
Do use prefers-reduced-motion to disable all animations for users who need it.
Do express depth through shadow vocabulary and color-step layering (Crypt to Walnut to Surface).
donts: Don't use white cards, blue primary actions, Inter/system fonts, or generic SaaS UI patterns.
Don't use children's fantasy aesthetics: bright primary colors, rounded bubble fonts, decorative illustrations that consume functional space.
Don't build anything that feels like a spreadsheet wearing a costume.
Don't apply the amber glow shadow to neutral cards or buttons at rest. The glow signals attuned items, loading states, and confirmed selections only.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards, list items, or callouts.
Don't apply background-clip: text with a gradient to create gradient text. Use Amber Wax Seal in solid form.
Don't add decoration to surfaces that are performing their function. Atmosphere comes from typography, color layering, and proportion.
Don't nest cards. A card inside a card is always a layout failure.
Don't reorder the six sections in DESIGN.md or rename their headers.
